plan_adherence_notes: The plan called for adding shared auth/rate-limit helpers and route-breadth HTTP tests in tests/test_bayes_market_api.py, then running that slice and only touching backend/server.py if the tests exposed a mismatch. Diffing 66c1bada..HEAD shows only .fleet task artifacts changed; there are no committed edits to tests/test_bayes_market_api.py or backend/server.py, so none of execution steps 1-5 were carried out.
reason: The research and source audit are sound: resolve_write_route_category(...) identifies the five protected POST routes, resolve_write_request_agent(...) and BayesHandler._enforce_write_controls() provide one pre-routing enforcement path, and BayesHandler.handle_api() is the only post-routing quota-header seam, with no route-local rate_limit_headers(...) writes. But the branch never executed the plan that was supposed to prove this with route-breadth HTTP coverage. Compared with ff/Tbayes-market-u44-execute-step-1, the branch only adds plan/research/question task files, leaving tests/test_bayes_market_api.py unchanged and still missing the planned comment/event-trade helper and all-five-route breadth assertions. python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market_api.py' passed (230 tests), but that only confirms the pre-existing suite, not the requested new coverage.
